LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CONSUMERINFO.COM, INC.'S, A.K.A. EXPERIAN CONSUMER DIRECT'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PARAGRAPH 8 REFLECTS THAT NO MISREPRESENTATION WAS MADE AS TO THE CONTENT OF THE CONTRACT AT THE TIME PLAINTIFF SIGNED THE AGREEMENT. BASED ON THE ANALYSIS IN PACIFIC STATE BANK V GREENE, 110 CAL APP 4TH 375, THIS IS A CASE OF PROMISSORY FRAUD AND IS BARRED BY THE PAROL EVIDENCE RULE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-27-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION, SEPARATE STATEMENT (OR IN THE ALT, FOR SUMM. ADJ. OF ISSUES); COMPENDIUM OF EXHIBITS ISO MOTION FILED BY DEFENDANT CONSUMERINFO.COM, INC HEARING SET FOR FEB-01-2008 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CONSUMERINFO.COM, INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE DECISION IN PACIFIC STATE BANK V GREENE (2003) 110 CAL APP 4TH 375, 391-392 MAKES CLEAR THAT THE PENDERGRASS RULE APPLIES TO ACTIONS FOR RESCISSION. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT, IN PARTICULAR PARAGRAPHS 8, 14-17 AND 20 MAKE CLEAR THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BASED UPON PROMISSORY FRAUD. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE PAROLE EVIDENCE RULE FOR THE PROMISE IS CONTRARY TO THE SUBSEQUENT WRITTEN AGREEMENT. MR. CARLSON TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR NOV-29-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER RE: STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER
